methods.description: Procedure for assessing the chronic toxic effects of herbicides on the specific growth rate (SGR) of Chaetoceros muelleri over 72 hours using static exposure and flow cytometry for cell density measurement. This method follows the test procedure by Thomas et al. (ref 26) and is based on OECD Test No. 201 (ref 46).

steps.description: Initially, 15 mL of algae inoculum was taken from 4-day-old C. muelleri culture (approximately 2 × 10 6 cells mL −1 ) in exponential growth phase
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain initial inoculum from exponential phase algal culture
inputs: 4-day-old C. muelleri culture (~2e6 cells/mL, exponential phase)

steps.description: washed with 15 mL sterile FSW by centrifugation in 50 mL falcon tubes at 1500×g for 5 min (Eppendorf Centrifuge 5810 R, Bio-strategy).
steps.searchable_summary: Wash inoculum with sterile FSW using centrifugation

steps.description: The supernatant was decanted
steps.searchable_summary: Decant supernatant after centrifugation

steps.description: and the remaining algae pellet homogenized in 30 mL FSW by vortexing.
steps.searchable_summary: Homogenize cell pellet in FSW by vortexing

steps.description: The centrifugation process was repeated three times prior to the start of each toxicity test.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat wash cycle (centrifugation, decanting, homogenization) three times

steps.description: After the final washing, the cell pellet was re-suspended in 15 mL of sterile 0.5 µm-FSW
steps.searchable_summary: Re-suspend final washed cell pellet in sterile FSW

steps.description: and the cell density of the concentrated algae suspension was measured from two 500 µL sub-samples by flow cytometry.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure cell density of concentrated suspension using flow cytometry from sub-samples

steps.description: The desired inoculum was calculated to have a starting cell density of 3 × 10 3 cells mL −1 in the toxicity tests.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate required inoculum volume for target starting cell density
inputs: Measured cell density of concentrated suspension

steps.description: Individual C. muelleri working suspensions for each herbicide treatment were prepared in 100 mL Schott glass bottles by adding the required algae inoculum and sterile 0.5 µm-FSW.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare working suspensions by adding calculated inoculum and FSW to test bottles

steps.description: Each Schott glass bottle was finally dosed with a range of herbicide concentrations
steps.searchable_summary: Dose test bottles with a range of herbicide concentrations

steps.description: Cell densities were obtained by plotting a two-dimensional cytogram. A fixed gating was used around the viable (chlorophyll fluorescing) cells, which allowed for differentiation of non-algal particles (debris) and dead cells from viable cells... Aliquots were run in duplicates and an average taken of the number of events that occurred within the gated region. This process was then repeated for each replicate per treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure cell density in test solutions at time points using flow cytometry with specific settings (35 µL/min flow, 16-µm core, 50 µL sample, fixed gating for viable cells, duplicates, average)
inputs: Dosed test solutions (at specified time points)
Flow cytometry settings (35 µL/min flow rate, 16-µm core size, 50 µL sample volume, fixed gating)
outputs: Measured cell densities (average counts/volume) for each treatment and time point

steps.description: Specific growth rates (SGR) were expressed as the logarithmic increase in cell density from day i (t i ) to day j (t j ) as per Eq. (1), where SGR i-j is the specific growth rate from time i to j; X j is the cell density at day j and X i is the cell density at day i 46 : SGR i-j = (ln(X j ) - ln(X i )) / (t j - t i )
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate specific growth rate (SGR) from cell density measurements over time using the provided formula
inputs: Cell densities (X_i, X_j) at time points (t_i, t_j)

steps.description: SGR relative to the control treatment was used to derive chronic effect values (EC 10 and EC 50 ) and no effect concentrations (NEC) for growth inhibition.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate SGR relative to the control treatment SGR

steps.description: SGR relative to the control treatment was used to derive chronic effect values (EC 10 and EC 50 ) and no effect concentrations (NEC) for growth inhibition.
steps.searchable_summary: Derive chronic effect values (EC10, EC50) and no effect concentrations (NEC) from dose-response data (relative SGR vs. concentration)

steps.description: A test was considered valid if the mean SGR of control replicates was ≥ 0.92 day −1 , the percent coefficient of variation (% CV) of the average specific growth rate of control cultures did not exceed 10% and the pH of the control medium did not increase by more than 1-unit during the test 46 .
steps.searchable_summary: Validate test based on control performance criteria (mean SGR, %CV of SGR, pH change)

methods.description: Procedure for measuring the effective quantum yield (ΔF/F m ′) of Chaetoceros muelleri using imaging PAM fluorometry after herbicide exposure, following a light:dark cycle. This method uses imaging PAM fluorometry (I-PAM, Walz, Germany) (ref 83, 90) and follows a single 12:12 h light:dark cycle (ref 26).

steps.description: following a single 12:12 h light:dark cycle (90-100 μmol photons m -2 s -1 ) 26 .
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate cell cultures under specified light/dark cycle and intensity
inputs: Light intensity setting (90-100 μmol photons m -2 s -1 )

steps.description: Light-adapted minimum fluorescence (F) and maximum fluorescence measurements (F m ′) were taken in 48-well plates (Nunclon Delta, Thermo Scientific)
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer cell samples to 48-well plates for measurement

steps.description: Light-adapted minimum fluorescence (F) ... measurements (F m ′) were taken ... using imaging PAM fluorometry (I-PAM, Walz, Germany) 83,90
steps.searchable_summary: Measure light-adapted minimum fluorescence (F) using imaging PAM fluorometry

steps.description: Light-adapted ... maximum fluorescence measurements (F m ′) were taken ... using imaging PAM fluorometry (I-PAM, Walz, Germany) 83,90
steps.searchable_summary: Measure light-adapted maximum fluorescence (Fm') using imaging PAM fluorometry

steps.description: from which the effective quantum yield was calculated as per Eq. (2) 90 . ΔF/F m ′ = (F m ′ - F) / F m ′
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate effective quantum yield (ΔF/Fm') from F and Fm' values using the provided formula

steps.description: An initial cell density of approximately 1 × 10 6 cells mL −1 was used to obtain ΔF/F m ′ measurements > 0.45
steps.searchable_summary: Adjust initial cell density to approximately 1e6 cells/mL for optimal measurement range
inputs: Cell density measurement method (e.g., flow cytometry, hemocytometer)

steps.description: with the following I-PAM settings: actinic light = 1 (corresponding to photosynthetically active radiation (PAR) of 100-110 μmol photons m -2 s -1 ), measuring intensity = 9, gain = 1; damp = 2.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure imaging PAM fluorometer settings (actinic light, measuring intensity, gain, damp)
inputs: Specified parameter values (actinic light=1, measuring intensity=9, gain=1, damp=2)

steps.description: Prior to herbicide exposure a screening process of control treatments was performed to ensure consistent ΔF/F m ′ measurements > 0.45.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen control samples before herbicide exposure to ensure consistent baseline fluorescence yield
outputs: Baseline ΔF/F m ′ data for controls

steps.description: Diuron was used as a referent toxicant (4 µg L −1 ) to monitor inhibition response between replicated algae cultures.
steps.searchable_summary: Include a reference toxicant (Diuron at 4 µg/L) treatment to monitor inhibition response consistency
outputs: Inhibition response data for reference toxicant
